claim: Agentic RAG allows agents to reason about which knowledge source to query instead of pre-fetching context
actionable: Build RAG systems where agents choose whether to search vector database, knowledge graph, or both based on query intent rather than forcing all contexts upfront.
source_title: Introducing RAG 2.0: Agentic RAG + Knowledge Graphs (FREE Template)

claim: Combine vector search (for single-entity lookup) with knowledge graphs (for relationship queries) in the same system
actionable: Store same data in both vector database and knowledge graph; use vector search for fact retrieval and graph queries for relationship analysis.
source_title: Introducing RAG 2.0: Agentic RAG + Knowledge Graphs (FREE Template)

claim: Knowledge graph insertion is computationally expensive; vector database insertion is fast (few seconds vs 2+ minutes)
actionable: Plan for longer ingest times when building knowledge graphs from documents; querying is fast but initial construction requires LLM calls for entity/relationship extraction.
source_title: Introducing RAG 2.0: Agentic RAG + Knowledge Graphs (FREE Template)

claim: System prompts must explicitly define when agents should use vector search vs graph search vs both
actionable: In agent system prompts, specify decision rules: use vector search for single-entity queries, graph search for relationships, hybrid for complex questions.
source_title: Introducing RAG 2.0: Agentic RAG + Knowledge Graphs (FREE Template)

claim: Use embedding models with consistent dimensions across your pipeline or ensure dimension consistency in SQL schemas
actionable: If changing embedding models, update vector dimensions in all database schemas (text-embed-3-small = 1536, others vary); document this requirement.
source_title: Introducing RAG 2.0: Agentic RAG + Knowledge Graphs (FREE Template)

claim: Use custom system prompts to control agent behavior; prompt and tools define when different retrieval methods are invoked
actionable: Customize your agent's system prompt to describe conditions for each tool; avoid relying on LLM guessing about when to use vector search vs graph queries.
source_title: Introducing RAG 2.0: Agentic RAG + Knowledge Graphs (FREE Template)

claim: AI coding assistants can handle complex project planning, database setup, and initial implementation if given MCP servers for context
actionable: When using Claude Code, provide MCP servers for external services (Neon for SQL, documentation crawlers); let agents handle database setup and boilerplate.
source_title: Introducing RAG 2.0: Agentic RAG + Knowledge Graphs (FREE Template)

claim: Context caching on LLM judge prompts can save 50-90% on output token costs by reusing the same rubric prefix
actionable: Structure LLM judge calls to put the rubric first (shared prefix), then the different outputs to judge; enables cache hits and cost savings.
source_title: Introducing RAG 2.0: Agentic RAG + Knowledge Graphs (FREE Template)
